ucs.title: [LLM] Salt Typhoon (Earth Estries / FamousSparrow) SparrowDoor / Crowdoor DLL side-load via signed AV/IT binaries
ucs.description: Hunts the Salt Typhoon side-loading pattern documented by Trend Micro (Earth Estries) and ESET (FamousSparrow): legitimately-signed third-party binaries (e.g. K7Computing K7Sysmon.exe, IDMU, BDReinit, Mc.exe, GUP.exe) executing from user-writable paths and loading attacker-supplied DLLs co-located in the same folder — the dropper for SparrowDoor, Crowdoor, ShadowPad and HemiGate. Highly specific because Salt Typhoon ships these vendor binaries as the loader stub and drops the malicious DLL alongside in %ProgramData%, %Public% or a fake C:\Windows sub-folder.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("K7Sysmon.exe","K7TSecurity.exe","IDMU.exe","BDReinit.exe","Mc.exe","GUP.exe","vfhost.exe","oleview.exe","VMwareXferlogs.exe","AUG.exe","AppLaunch.exe") AND NOT (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\Program Files\\*","*\\Program Files (x86)\\*","*\\Windows\\System32\\*","*\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.process Processes.parent_process_name | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest process_name [| tstats summariesonly=t values(Filesystem.file_name) as sideload_dll values(Filesystem.file_path) as sideload_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name="*.dll" AND NOT (Filesystem.file_path IN ("*\\Program Files\\*","*\\Windows\\System32\\*","*\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\*")) by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_path | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)` | rename process_name as process_name] | where like(sideload_path, process_path."%") OR like(sideload_path, substr(process_path,1,len(process_path)-len(process_name))."%") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Salt Typhoon SparrowDoor / Crowdoor / ShadowPad side-load loaders
let SaltTyphoonLoaderBins = dynamic([
    "K7Sysmon.exe","K7TSecurity.exe","K7TSMain.exe",  // K7Computing — Crowdoor/SparrowDoor (Earth Estries)
    "IDMU.exe",                                          // SparrowDoor sideload host
    "BDReinit.exe",                                      // Bitdefender stub abused by Earth Estries
    "Mc.exe","GUP.exe","vfhost.exe",                    // McAfee / Notepad++ updater / VFhost stubs
    "oleview.exe","VMwareXferlogs.exe",                 // Microsoft / VMware signed sideload hosts
    "AUG.exe","AppLaunch.exe","javaw.exe"               // ShadowPad / HemiGate sideload hosts
]);
let WritableDirs = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Intel\", @"\Windows\Temp\"]);
let Loaders = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where FileName in~ (SaltTyphoonLoaderBins)
    | where FolderPath has_any (WritableDirs)                           // signed binary running from non-default path
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | project LoaderTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              LoaderName = FileName, LoaderPath = FolderPath,
              LoaderSHA256 = SHA256, LoaderCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              LoaderProcessId = ProcessId;
DeviceImageLoadEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where FileName endswith ".dll"
| where FolderPath has_any (WritableDirs)
| join kind=inner Loaders on DeviceId, $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.LoaderProcessId
| where Timestamp between (LoaderTime .. LoaderTime + 5m)
| where FolderPath =~ LoaderPath                                         // DLL co-located with loader = side-load shape
| where SHA256 != LoaderSHA256
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          LoaderName, LoaderPath, LoaderCmd, LoaderSHA256,
          SideloadDll = FileName, SideloadPath = FolderPath, SideloadSHA256 = SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Salt Typhoon SparrowDoor / Crowdoor side-load — Sentinel via SecurityEvent 4688
let SaltTyphoonLoaderBins = dynamic([
    "K7Sysmon.exe","K7TSecurity.exe","K7TSMain.exe","IDMU.exe","BDReinit.exe",
    "Mc.exe","GUP.exe","vfhost.exe","oleview.exe","VMwareXferlogs.exe",
    "AUG.exe","AppLaunch.exe","javaw.exe"
]);
let WritableDirs = dynamic([@"\ProgramData\", @"\Users\Public\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\PerfLogs\", @"\Intel\", @"\Windows\Temp\"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend BinName = tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1])
| where BinName in~ (SaltTyphoonLoaderBins)
| where NewProcessName has_any (WritableDirs)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, BinName, NewProcessName, CommandLine, ParentProcessName, NewProcessId
| join kind=leftouter (
    ImFileEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d)
    | where EventType == "FileCreated"
    | where TargetFileName endswith ".dll"
    | where TargetFilePath has_any (WritableDirs)
    | project DropTime = TimeGenerated, Computer = DvcHostname,
              SideloadDll = TargetFileName, SideloadPath = TargetFilePath,
              SideloadSHA256 = TargetFileSHA256, ActingProcessName
  ) on Computer
| where DropTime between (TimeGenerated - 30m .. TimeGenerated + 5m)
| where tostring(split(SideloadPath, "\\")[-2]) == tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-2])
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, BinName, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          SideloadDll, SideloadPath, SideloadSHA256
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.rationale: Earth Estries / FamousSparrow / GhostEmperor (= Salt Typhoon, MITRE G1045) ships its custom backdoors — SparrowDoor, Crowdoor, ShadowPad, HemiGate — almost exclusively via DLL side-loading off legitimately-signed third-party binaries (K7Computing, McAfee, Bitdefender, IDMU, VMware), staged in user-writable directories. The dual-condition shape (signed loader binary in ProgramData/Public + co-locat

ucs.title: [LLM] Salt Typhoon LSASS credential extraction via rundll32 + comsvcs.dll MiniDump
ucs.description: Hunts the comsvcs.dll MiniDump invocation Salt Typhoon uses immediately after edge-device / Exchange exploitation to harvest cached credentials and Kerberos tickets from telecom and government targets. Salt Typhoon prefers this LOLBin path over Mimikatz to evade AV — `rundll32.exe C:\Windows\System32\comsvcs.dll MiniDump <lsass_pid> <out.dmp> full`.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=t count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name="rundll32.exe" OR Processes.process_name="powershell.exe" OR Processes.process_name="pwsh.exe" OR Processes.process_name="cmd.exe") AND Processes.process="*comsvcs*" AND Processes.process="*MiniDump*" by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where NOT match(user, "(?i).*\$$") | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
// Salt Typhoon — comsvcs.dll MiniDump LSASS dump (post-exploit credential access)
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where AccountName !endswith "$"
| where (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
         and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs"
         and ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDump")
     or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
         and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs"
         and ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDump")
     or (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe"
         and ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)#\s*\+?\s*24\b")   // ordinal #24 == MiniDump
| extend TargetPid = extract(@"MiniDump\s+(\d+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine),
         DumpPath = extract(@"MiniDump\s+\d+\s+([^\s]+)", 1, ProcessCommandLine)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
          FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentImage = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          ParentCmd  = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          ParentSHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
          IntegrityLevel = ProcessIntegrityLevel,
          IsRemote = AdditionalFields,
          TargetPid, DumpPath, SHA256
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
// Salt Typhoon — comsvcs.dll MiniDump LSASS dump (Sentinel via 4688)
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30d)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend BinName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where (BinName == "rundll32.exe"
         and CommandLine has "comsvcs"
         and CommandLine has "MiniDump")
     or (BinName in ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe")
         and CommandLine has "comsvcs"
         and CommandLine has "MiniDump")
     or (BinName == "rundll32.exe" and CommandLine matches regex @"(?i)#\s*\+?\s*24\b")
| extend TargetPid = extract(@"MiniDump\s+(\d+)", 1, CommandLine),
         DumpPath  = extract(@"MiniDump\s+\d+\s+([^\s]+)", 1, CommandLine)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType, TargetPid, DumpPath
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Salt Typhoon LSASS Credential Dump via comsvcs.dll MiniDump
id: 8a3c5e72-7f2f-4a3e-9d7f-saltlsass001
status: experimental
description: Detects rundll32 / scripting host invoking comsvcs.dll MiniDump export to dump LSASS — Salt Typhoon (G1045) tradecraft for harvesting credentials from telco/government victims post-edge-exploit.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1045/
  - https://www.trendmicro.com/en_us/research/24/k/earth-estries.html
  - https://www.welivesecurity.com/en/eset-research/famoussparrow-resurfaces-with-crowdoor/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/05
tags:
  - attack.credential_access
  - attack.t1003.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1218.011
  - detection.salt_typhoon
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_proc:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
  selection_cmd:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'comsvcs'
      - 'MiniDump'
  selection_ordinal:
    Image|endswith: '\rundll32.exe'
    CommandLine|re: '(?i)comsvcs(\.dll)?[\s,]+#\s*24\b'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_proc and (selection_cmd or selection_ordinal) and not filter_machine_acct
fields:
  - Image
  - CommandLine
  - ParentImage
  - User
  - Computer
falsepositives:
  - Authorised IR / forensic memory captures (rare; should be allowlisted by host)
level: high
ucs.rationale: comsvcs.dll MiniDump is heavily preferred by Salt Typhoon (a.k.a. Earth Estries / FamousSparrow / GhostEmperor — MITRE G1045) over Mimikatz because it avoids dropping signature-known tooling and rides a Microsoft-signed LOLBin — exactly fitting their fileless / signed-binary preference also seen in the Demodex rootkit and SparrowDoor loader chains. Microsoft and Trend Micro both call out comsvcs M
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
